pages.raw_text:
The official party from Washing
ton, d. c., that arrived In Spokane
Saturday afternoon late broke up
last night Secretary OF the Interior
James r. Garfield leaving for Seat
tie accompanied by DIR. w. J. Nc
Gee, secretary of the inland water
ways commission; Prof. J. A.
Holmes, Of the geological survey.
and Secretary Garfleld's son. John.
Chief Forester Pinchot left for
Moscow this morning, intending
later to join Secretary Garfield on
the coast.


Saturday evening the party was
formally welcomed to Spokane with
a banquet at Davenport's by repre-
sentative business men of the city.
Mayor Moore acted as sponsor for
Secretary Garfield, introducing him
In a brief address on the value OF
the work of interior department.
The secretary expressed a desire to
get in touch with western people
and interests as the chief object OF
his trip and talked at length ON
his departments operations in pro-
tecting government timber and
lands and coal deposits. He was
sent out to reconcile this section OF
the country to the administration
forest reserve policy and the adj
dress of Mr.. Pinchot, who followed
Secretary Garfield. was devoted to
this policy. DR.. MCGEE spoke ON
the preservation of water power,
Prof Holmes on coal deposits, and
brief addresses were made by SEN
ator Ankeny and Congressman
Jones. The latter declared belief
that the people Of this state support
the forest reserve policy.


On Sunday. the visitors were
given q view of the Spokane valley
by an automobile trip to Hayden
lake. Jay p. Graves auto led the
expedition with Secretary Garfield
and the balance OF the party was
distributed among the delegation OF
members OF the chamber Of COM
merce in 5 other automobiles. Es-
pecial attention was given the irri-
cation projects now In operation In
the valley.
